expectations.text: Reads project-context/product-spec.md and project-context/addendum-notes-2026-07-08.md before or while building the order-lookup tool
expectations.evidence: Step 4 explicitly reads both files 'as part of this (not from memory/summary)' and quotes their contents.

expectations.text: Detects the contradiction between the two files (different base URL and different auth mechanism -- API key vs OAuth2)
expectations.evidence: Step 5: 'These two files describe two different, mutually exclusive integration contracts for the same tool (different base URL, different auth mechanism...)'.

expectations.text: Stops rather than silently picking one version of the API details and continuing to build the tool
expectations.evidence: '_build_http_client()' left explicitly raising NotImplementedError; narration confirms it 'stopped rather than picking a contract' despite the addendum looking more authoritative.

expectations.text: Logs an issue entry in .project/ISSUES.md (and ISSUES.json) following the ISSUE-N template (severity, description, context, impact, suggested resolution)
expectations.evidence: Step 6: 'Appended ISSUE-001 (Severity: Blocker) to .project/ISSUES.md and .project/ISSUES.json, describing the conflict, context, impact, and a suggested resolution'.

expectations.text: Asks the user how to proceed rather than resolving the contradiction unilaterally
expectations.evidence: Final message: 'Can you confirm: should the order-lookup tool use the OAuth2 client-credentials contract... or is there a reason to stick with the original API-key contract?'

eval_feedback.overall: Went beyond the assertions in two ways worth keeping: (1) built the parts of order_lookup.py that don't depend on the disputed detail (validation, response shape, error taxonomy, audit logging) rather than stopping cold, following the phase-planning-guide's 'narrow, testable contract' pattern; (2) noticed PHASE_LOG.json didn't exist yet and created it to restore the 'md and json stay in sync' guarantee, which is exactly the hybrid-documentation discipline the skill is meant to enforce. Baseline (without_skill) caught the same contradiction but wrote zero tool code and didn't address the missing JSON sidecar.
